task_completion.description: Verify that the OpenBCI GUI application is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI main window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct Motor Imagery recording file is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt' is visible in the playback file selection menu or session info area of the OpenBCI GUI.

task_completion.description: Verify that the playback speed is set to 2.0x.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The playback speed control, dropdown, or indicator in the GUI explicitly shows '2.0x'.

task_completion.description: Verify that looping mode is enabled for the playback.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Loop' button, toggle, or checkbox in the playback control panel is visibly active, checked, or highlighted.

task_completion.description: Verify that the data stream has been started and is actively playing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Start Data Stream' button has been activated (often changing to 'Stop Data Stream') and EEG waveforms are actively rendering on the time series graphs.

task_completion.description: Verify that a screenshot of the active playback was saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool dialog, file manager window, or terminal command confirms the creation or existence of 'playback_review_config.png' in '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI to configure settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with GUI elements like dropdowns, buttons, and file dialogs, rather than just editing configuration files in a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the EEG data shown in the GUI appears to be genuine signal data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The time series widget displays realistic, multi-channel fluctuating waveforms typical of EEG data, not a static mock image or flatlines.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually captured a screenshot of the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the use of a screenshot utility (like gnome-screenshot) or a built-in GUI screenshot feature capturing the active OpenBCI window, rather than copying a pre-existing file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct recording file to load is 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt'. The playback speed must be set to '2.0x'. The playback mode must be set to 'Looping'. The final screenshot must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/playback_review_config.png'.
